title: Index-based 'for' loops over a collection should prefer 'foreach'
messageFormat: Use a 'foreach' loop to iterate this collection instead of an index-based 'for'.
summary: A for loop that starts at zero, runs while the counter is below a collection's Length or Count, increments by one, and only uses the counter to index that collection is a manual foreach. The index adds an off-by-one risk and obscures the simple intent of visiting each element.
When the index itself is not needed, foreach states the loop's purpose directly and cannot run past the end of the sequence.
